loops._spawn_isolation_note: Loop body runs inline in the main agent (Opus) for quality; the Step-3 implementation reviewer and any HALT_SUCCESS challenger are still spawned as independent subagents, preserving both independence-critical gates.
loops.verdict_explanation: The tool works correctly and the code is honest — no fake architecture, no misleading abstractions — but its central data carrier is an anonymous 11-field positional tuple, its search engine is a single ~720-line function that fuses transport, parsing, filtering, date inference and terminal rendering, and there are zero automated tests. Ownership is clean and the program is trivially concurrency-safe (synchronous), so the weaknesses are structural (domain modeling, decomposition, regression resistance), not correctness or safety.

architecture_quality.proof: search_boards fuses transport (soundboard-snag.py:787,855), regex parsing (860-903), filtering (1046-1100), date inference (962-1044) and rendering (1105-1141,1268-1334) in one function

state_management.proof: single-threaded; SoundboardSnag attrs immutable post-__init__ (soundboard-snag.py:290-293); search_boards locals have one writer each; no multi-writer or hidden state machines

domain_modeling.proof: no domain types pre-refactor; central record was an anonymous 11-tuple (soundboard-snag.py:1145 pre-loop), sounds_info is bare 2-tuples, approx_source is stringly-typed

data_flow.proof: positional 11-tuple was an implicit unenforced contract across construction + 3 read sites; main re-implements result filtering (soundboard-snag.py:1525) duplicating search_boards' own filter (1181)

framework_idioms.proof: idiomatic stdlib use (with urlopen, argparse, os.path); _sanitize_filename (soundboard-snag.py:383-441) is genuinely defensive — neutralizes path traversal, reserved names, control chars; HTTPS BASE_URL; no secrets

concurrency.proof: no async/threading/multiprocessing imports; purely synchronous CLI — no shared mutable concurrency, no reentrancy, no task lifetime to manage
concurrency.residual_blocking_10: time.sleep pacing (soundboard-snag.py:595,1156,1176) is the only timing; intentional server etiquette, not a determinism hazard
concurrency.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: Tool is deliberately synchronous (zero-dependency stdlib CLI). There is no concurrency model to make safer; the sleeps are network etiquette, not a hazard. Permanent design carve-out.

simplicity.proof: one ~720-line function (soundboard-snag.py:612-1334) ~6 levels deep; date-display render duplicated near-verbatim (1121-1136 vs 1286-1301); dead wrapper _fetch_last_modified (223-226)

test_strategy.proof: zero test files; deterministic network-free helpers untested — _sanitize_filename (383), _parse_views_count (94), _parse_http_datetime (163); a suffix-flip mutation at _parse_views_count:118-121 would not be caught

credibility.proof: code is honest (does what it claims, no fake-clean), but a senior reviewer reading in source order hits the 11-tuple (1145) and 720-line engine (612) and zero tests, undercutting architectural confidence

strengths: Filename sanitization is genuinely defensive, not cosmetic — soundboard-snag.py:383-441 decodes HTML entities, strips UUID patterns, neutralizes path traversal (/ and \ replaced), control chars, and Windows reserved names from untrusted header/title input
Honest synchronous design: zero shared-concurrency hazards because there is no concurrency — REQUEST_DELAY pacing (soundboard-snag.py:595,1156,1176) is deliberate server etiquette
Zero third-party dependencies held to genuinely (stdlib-only imports, soundboard-snag.py:60-72), matching the project's stated constraint

findings.title: search_boards returns an anonymous 11-field positional tuple
findings.why_it_matters: The primary data carrier of the search engine forces every caller to know that index 8 is views_int and index 9 is approx_updated; reordering the tuple breaks all callers silently — a hazard the project's own CLAUDE.md explicitly warns about.
findings.what_is_wrong: search_boards builds and returns 11-element bare tuples that callers read by position (r[1], x[8], x[9], results[0][1]) and by an 11-name destructure repeated in two render loops; the field meaning lives only in a docstring.
findings.leverage_impact: Callers learn far too much (positional layout of 11 fields) for what the interface delivers.
findings.locality_impact: A field reorder or insertion forces coordinated edits at every read site with no compiler or test to catch a miss.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Anonymous positional tuples as the central domain record are the textbook weak-domain-model smell; they make the search engine's output fragile and hard to read.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Introduce a BoardResult typing.NamedTuple (Python 3.6+ compatible; still a tuple at runtime so the change is behavior-preserving) and convert every construction and read site to named-field access.

findings.title: search_boards is a ~720-line function fusing transport, parsing, filtering, date inference and rendering
findings.why_it_matters: The core scraping logic cannot be tested or reused because it is welded to network I/O and terminal printing, so regression resistance and architecture both suffer.
findings.what_is_wrong: A single function (soundboard-snag.py:612-1334) performs HTTP fetching, regex HTML extraction, domain filtering, best-effort date inference, sorting and all terminal rendering, interleaved inside one deeply nested loop.
findings.leverage_impact: Maintainers must hold the entire function in their head to change any one concern.
findings.locality_impact: A parsing change risks the rendering and filtering paths because they share one scope and 20+ locals.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Fusing pure parsing with I/O and presentation is the primary reason the codebase has no unit tests — the testable logic is unreachable without the network.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Extract the pure per-board HTML->BoardResult parsing into a module-level function that takes html text and returns the data, leaving transport and rendering in search_boards; this makes the parse unit-testable without network. Prove friction first (done: no parse test can exist today). Do over multiple loops; do not introduce a class hierarchy.

findings.title: Zero automated tests; deterministic helpers untested; hyphenated module name blocks import
findings.why_it_matters: Multi-branch pure logic (filename sanitization, views parsing, HTTP date parsing) can regress silently with no signal, and the file name itself obstructs even writing a test.
findings.what_is_wrong: No test files exist. Network-free, deterministic functions with non-trivial branching are untested, and the module file is named soundboard-snag.py (a hyphen is not a valid identifier) so it cannot be imported without importlib gymnastics.
findings.leverage_impact: Without tests at the helper interfaces, every refactor of those helpers is unverified.
findings.locality_impact: A sanitization regression would corrupt output filenames across every download with no failing test.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Absence of tests on stateful/parsing logic is the binding regression-resistance gap and caps the test-strategy dimension.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Add test_soundboard_snag.py using stdlib unittest, loading the module via importlib.util.spec_from_file_location; cover _sanitize_filename, _parse_views_count, _parse_http_datetime, _extract_board_slugs_from_search_html with table-driven cases.

findings.title: Dead pass-through wrapper _fetch_last_modified
findings.why_it_matters: Unused indirection adds reading cost and a second name for one behavior with no caller to justify it.
findings.what_is_wrong: _fetch_last_modified wraps _fetch_last_modified_detailed and discards the diagnostic, but nothing in the module calls it; the live path uses _fetch_last_modified_detailed and the fetch_last_modified_cached closure.
findings.locality_impact: Minor: a reader must confirm it is unused before trusting the live path.
findings.why_weakens_submission: A pass-through wrapper with no caller is dead weight that the deletion test removes cleanly.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Delete _fetch_last_modified (soundboard-snag.py:223-226). Confirm debug_track_dates.py does not import it (it cannot — hyphenated module name).

findings.title: Date-display and skipped-breakdown rendering duplicated near-verbatim
findings.why_it_matters: Two copies of the same presentation logic must be kept in sync by hand; they will drift.
findings.what_is_wrong: The approx-updated date block and the skipped-buckets breakdown block each appear twice with near-identical bodies.
findings.leverage_impact: A formatting change must be made in two places.
findings.locality_impact: Drift between the two copies produces inconsistent output for the same data.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Duplicate presentation logic is a simplification deduction and compounds the god-function problem in F2.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Once F2 begins separating rendering from searching, extract the two duplicated blocks into small module-level format helpers that take a BoardResult and the date-stats map; do not extract before the rendering is separated (avoid premature helper).

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: F1 BoardResult resolves the weak-domain-model / fragile-positional-contract finding — Shallow-module test now passes (interface carries named fields, not positions). Behavior-preserving because NamedTuple is still a tuple.
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: F4 (delete dead _fetch_last_modified) and F5 (de-duplicate render blocks) are subtractive follow-ups.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not split the single file into a package, add a class hierarchy for parsing, or introduce a port/adapter for HTTP — the tool is intentionally a zero-dependency single-file CLI and friction for a network seam is not proven (one production transport, no test fake planned).
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: BoardResult is a domain type; its index+attr compatibility and sort-key usage were smoke-verified this loop. Dedicated helper tests land in F3 (next priority), where test_soundboard_snag.py will assert sanitization/parsing at the helper interfaces.

backlog.title: Replace search_boards' 11-field positional tuple with a BoardResult NamedTuple (F1)
backlog.why_it_matters: Biggest single readability/robustness gain; removes the silent-reorder hazard CLAUDE.md warns about.

backlog.title: Add a stdlib unittest suite for the pure helpers (F3)
backlog.why_it_matters: Test strategy is the binding constraint; sanitization/parsing have branchy logic that can regress silently.

backlog.title: Extract pure per-board HTML parsing out of search_boards (F2)
backlog.why_it_matters: Makes the core scraping logic testable and shrinks the god-function.

deepening_candidates.source_friction_proven: Finding F2 — the parse logic is unreachable for testing because it is fused with network I/O and printing
deepening_candidates.why_shallow_or_misplaced: There is no parsing interface at all; the behavior is inlined in a 720-line function so it has neither Depth nor a Seam.
deepening_candidates.behavior_to_move_behind_interface: Regex extraction of board name, downloads flag, sound ids/titles, description, category, views, tags from one board's HTML, returning a BoardResult (minus the network-derived approx date).
deepening_candidates.test_surface_after_change: test_soundboard_snag.py asserts parsing on saved HTML fixtures without network.
deepening_candidates.smallest_first_step: Extract _parse_board_html(html, board_name) returning the parsed fields; call it from the loop.
deepening_candidates.what_not_to_do: Do not introduce a parser class hierarchy or a strategy pattern; one pure function is the honest shape.

builder_notes.pattern: Anonymous positional tuple used as a multi-field domain record
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: Callers read results via r[8] / x[9] or a long destructure, and the field meaning lives only in a docstring or comment.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: The moment a tuple has 3+ heterogeneous fields read at more than one site, make it a NamedTuple — it is index-compatible so the change is free.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: typing.NamedTuple gives attribute access and stays a tuple at runtime, so existing indexing/unpacking keeps working during the migration.

builder_notes.pattern: God-function fusing transport, computation and presentation
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: One function owns 15+ mutable locals, makes network calls, parses data, and prints — and you cannot unit-test any slice of it without the network.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Pull the pure computation (parse/filter/format) out as functions that take data and return data; let the I/O function orchestrate.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: a _parse_board_html(html)->record function is testable against a saved fixture; the network loop just calls it.

builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A thin function delegates to another and drops a return value, but grep finds no call site.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Run the deletion test before keeping any wrapper: if removing it makes complexity vanish rather than reappear across callers, delete it.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: _fetch_last_modified delegating to _fetch_last_modified_detailed with zero callers.

loops.narrative: Place — functionally solid but structurally compromised, and the gap is entirely structural rather than correctness or safety. Ownership is trustworthy (single-threaded, one writer per concern) and concurrency is trustworthy because there is no concurrency. The three deductions that matter are the anonymous 11-tuple domain record (addressed this loop), the 720-line search engine that fuses I/O with logic, and the complete absence of tests. This loop's simplification (named BoardResult) helped and added no ceremony. The dominant risk in future loops is over-reaching toward a package split or HTTP seam the single-file zero-dependency design does not warrant; future work should stay subtractive and add tests, not layers.

loop_result.what_changed: Introduced a BoardResult typing.NamedTuple in soundboard-snag.py and converted search_boards' result construction plus every read site (filter, both sort keys, the results-render loop, the download-suggestion line, and main's search-and-download loop) from positional tuple access to named-field access.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m py_compile soundboard-snag.py passes; an importlib smoke test confirms BoardResult is index-compatible (br[1] is br.has_downloads), attribute access works, and the views_int/approx_updated sort keys still order correctly; --help exits 0; grep confirms no positional access of search results remains (L1255 is an unrelated 2-tuple sort, L94 is the new docstring).

implementation_review.reason: All three checks pass: F1's positional access pattern is gone, the BoardResult NamedTuple is behavior-preserving (still a plain tuple at runtime, constructed by keyword so no transposition risk), and no new finding at same-or-higher severity was introduced.

loops._spawn_isolation_note: Loop body inline in main (Opus); Step-3 reviewer + HALT challenger spawned as independent subagents.
loops.verdict_explanation: After loop 1 the central record is a named BoardResult, which lifts domain modeling and data flow. The remaining structural gaps are the ~734-line search_boards function that still fuses transport, parsing, filtering, dates and rendering, and (at the start of this loop) the absence of any tests. This loop adds the missing test surface for the pure helpers.

state_management.proof: single-threaded; one writer per concern; SoundboardSnag attrs immutable post-__init__

domain_modeling.proof: BoardResult NamedTuple introduced (soundboard-snag.py:89-110, commit afb1ab6) — the central record now names its fields

data_flow.proof: positional 11-tuple contract removed (commit afb1ab6); all read sites use named fields. Residual: main still re-filters results (soundboard-snag.py:~1560) duplicating search_boards' own filter

framework_idioms.proof: idiomatic stdlib; _sanitize_filename defensive (383-441 region); HTTPS; no secrets

concurrency.proof: no async/threading — synchronous CLI, no shared-mutable hazard
concurrency.residual_blocking_10: time.sleep pacing is intentional server etiquette, not a determinism hazard
concurrency.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: Tool is deliberately synchronous; no concurrency model to make safer. Permanent design carve-out.

simplicity.proof: named field reads removed positional opacity at read sites (commit afb1ab6); but ~734-line function (635-1369), duplicated render (1159 vs 1336; 1249 vs 1361) and dead wrapper (246) remain

test_strategy.proof: at loop-2 start there were still zero tests; the suite is this loop's fix (reported in loop_result, scored next loop)

credibility.proof: BoardResult domain type makes the search engine's output self-describing (commit afb1ab6); code remains honest with no fake-clean

strengths: Loop 1's BoardResult is a genuine deepening: the search engine's output now names its 11 fields with zero behavioral change (still a tuple at runtime)
Filename sanitization remains a real defensive strength against untrusted header/title input (soundboard-snag.py:_sanitize_filename)
Synchronous design keeps the concurrency surface trivially safe

builder_notes.pattern: Untestable logic because the file name is not importable
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: The module file has a hyphen or other non-identifier char, so `import x` fails and there is no test file at all.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Load by path with importlib.util.spec_from_file_location under a clean module name; loading runs only top-level definitions when main() is guarded by __name__ == '__main__'.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: spec_from_file_location('soundboard_snag', 'soundboard-snag.py') gives an importable handle to a hyphenated file.

builder_notes.pattern: Characterization tests before a risky refactor
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: You are about to extract or move logic that has no tests; a silent behavior change would go unnoticed.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Pin current observable behavior of the pure helpers first (including the non-obvious cases), then refactor against a green suite.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: assert _sanitize_filename('CON.mp3', ...) == '_Con.mp3' captures the exact title-case-then-reserve ordering before you touch it.

builder_notes.pattern: Test the pure surface, not the I/O surface
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: The tempting target is the big network function, but it needs heavy mocking; the real value is in the deterministic helpers it calls.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Test the network-free functions directly; only mock I/O after you have extracted the pure logic from it.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: test _parse_views_count / _parse_http_datetime directly; defer testing search_boards until parsing is extracted.

loops.narrative: Place — still functionally solid and structurally improving. Loop 1's BoardResult lifted domain modeling, data flow, simplicity and credibility with no behavioral change. This loop closes the most glaring regression-resistance gap by adding a 24-case characterization suite over the pure helpers, loaded past the hyphenated-filename obstacle via importlib. Ownership and concurrency remain trustworthy. The next structural move is extracting per-board parsing from the 734-line search engine, now de-risked by the test suite; the standing risk is over-reaching toward a package split or HTTP seam the single-file design does not warrant.

loop_result.what_changed: Added test_soundboard_snag.py, a stdlib unittest suite of 24 characterization tests over the pure network-free helpers (_parse_views_count, _parse_http_datetime, _extract_board_slugs_from_search_html, _quote_path_segment, _extract_filename_from_headers, _sanitize_filename, the date formatters), loading the hyphenated module via importlib.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m py_compile passes on both files; python3 -m unittest test_soundboard_snag runs 24 tests, all OK; the assertions pin real current behavior including non-obvious cases (CON.mp3 -> _Con.mp3, audio_42 -> 'Audio 42.mp3', percent-escape preservation, UTC datetime conversion). No production code was touched, so behavior is unchanged by construction.

implementation_review.reason: All three checks pass: F-003 is resolved by a real 186-line test file that loads the hyphenated module via importlib and asserts behavior across all six named pure helpers plus date formatters; every spot-checked assertion reflects actual production code behavior; no production file was modified and the test suite makes no network calls.
